tex:
\begin{question*}{1}{a}Malena has 450 fruit trees.
The fruit trees are in the ratio apple : pear : plum = 8 : 7 : 3.

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Show that Malena has 200 apple trees.

Find the number of plum trees.

Malena wants to increase the number of pear trees by 32\%.

Calculate the number of extra pear trees she needs.

Each apple tree produces 48.5 kg of apples.
The apples have an average mass of 165 g each.

Calculate the total number of apples produced by the 200 trees.
Give your answer correct to the nearest 1000 apples.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)Malena's land is valued at three million and seventy-five thousand dollars.

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Write this number in figures.

Write your answer to part (b)(i) in standard form.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(c)In 2020, each plum tree produced 37.7 kg of plums.
This was 16\% more than in 2019.

Calculate the mass of plums produced by each plum tree in 2019.

(d)Malena invests \$1800 at a rate of 2.1\% per year compound interest.

Calculate the value of her investment at the end of 15 years.

\end{question*}

\begin{question*}{2}{a}The cumulative frequency diagram shows information about the floor area, a m$^2$, of each of
80 houses.

Use the diagram to find an estimate of

\begin{subpartsaliged}
the median,

the lower quartile,

the interquartile range,

the number of houses with a floor area greater than 120 m$^2$.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)The information about the 80 floor areas is shown in this frequency table.

Floor area
(a m$^2$)

40 $\le$ a $<$ 60 \quad 14 \\
60 $\le$ a $<$ 80 \quad 17 \\
80 $\le$ a $<$ 100 \quad 18 \\
100 $\le$ a $<$ 130 \quad 15 \\
130 $\le$ a $<$ 160 \quad 9 \\
160 $\le$ a $<$ 200 \quad 7

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Calculate an estimate of the mean floor area.

Complete the histogram to show the information in the frequency table.

Two of the houses are picked at random.

Find the probability that one of the houses has a floor area greater than 130 m$^2$ and the other
has a floor area 60 m$^2$ or less.

\end{subpartsaliged}

\end{question*}

\begin{question*}{7}{a}The diagram shows a container for storing grain.

The container is made from a hemisphere,
a cylinder and a cone, each with radius 2 m.
The height of the cylinder is 5.2 m and the
height of the cone is h m.

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Calculate the volume of the hemisphere.
Give your answer as a multiple of $\pi$.
[The volume, V, of a sphere with radius r is V = $\dfrac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.]

The total volume of the container is $\dfrac{88\pi}{3}$ m$^3$.
Calculate the value of h.
[The volume, V, of a cone with radius r and height h is V = $\dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$.]

h = 

The container is full of grain.
Grain is removed from the container at a rate of 35 000 kg per hour.
1 m$^3$ of grain has a mass of 620 kg.

Calculate the time taken to empty the container.
Give your answer in hours and minutes.

 h  min

\end{subpartsaliged}

A and B are points on a circle, centre O, radius r cm.

\begin{question*}{8}{a}Kaito runs along a 12 km path at an average speed of x km/h.

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Write down an expression, in terms of x, for the number of hours he takes.

 hours

Yuki takes 1.5 hours longer to walk along the same path as Kaito.
She walks at an average speed of (x - 4) km/h.

Write down an equation, in terms of x, and show that it simplifies to x$^2$ - 4x - 32 = 0.

Solve by factorisation.
x$^2$ - 4x - 32 = 0

x =  or x = 

Find the number of hours it takes Yuki to walk along the 12 km path.

 hours

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)A bus travels 440 km, correct to the nearest 10 km.
The time taken to complete the journey is 6 hours, correct to the nearest half hour.

Calculate the lower bound of the speed of the bus.

 km/h

\end{question*}

\begin{question*}{11}{a}

Triangle PQR is mathematically similar to triangle XYZ.

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Find YZ.

YZ =  cm

The area of triangle XYZ is 63.6 cm$^2$.

Calculate the area of triangle PQR.

 cm$^2$

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)Two containers are mathematically similar.
The larger container has a capacity of 64.8 litres and a surface area of 0.792 m$^2$.
The smaller container has a capacity of 37.5 litres.

Calculate the surface area of the smaller container.

 m$^2$
